messages.content: asume el papel de un nativo americano el cual chatea conmigo para ayudarme a mejorar mi ingles, soy nivel basico de ingles recien comenzando, debes chatear conmigo y seguir la conversacion y resaltar y corregir cualquier error que tenga en mis oraciones y luego seguir con la conversacion, debes usar palabras sencillas y practicas, el tema de la conversacion es un paseo en el parque

Hello! I'm glad to chat with you and help you with your English. A walk in the park sounds nice. Did you go to a park recently?

(Consider starting a simple conversation. For example, you can say: "I went to the park yesterday. It was fun.")
i didnt went to the park lastly

Almost perfect! Just a small correction: the correct way to say it is "I didn't go to the park recently." Remember to use "didn't" with the base form of the verb "go."

So, what do you like to do at the park?
walking through the way, feeling the fresh air and behold the environment

You're doing well! Just small corrections: It's better to say "walking through the path, feeling the fresh air, and beholding the environment." Here's why:

1. "Through the way" is not wrong, but "through the path" is a more common expression in English.
2. "Behold" is quite an old-fashioned word; you could simply say "seeing" or "looking at the environment."

So you can say: "I like walking through the path, feeling the fresh air, and looking at the environment."

What do you see when you walk in the park? Do you see any animals or flowers?
i see the other people and their pet dogs, and children plays with the grass

Good job! Here are a couple of small corrections to make it sound more natural:

Instead of "children plays with the grass," you would say, "children playing with the grass" or "children play in the grass."

So you can say: "I see other people and their pet dogs, and children playing in the grass."

Pets can be really fun to watch. Do you have a favorite type of dog?
none special, i like all dogs that i see

That's great! You can simply say, "No special favorite, I like all the dogs that I see."

It's wonderful that you enjoy all kinds of dogs. Do you ever play with them, or do you prefer just watching them?
I like playing with them, I like when they go for their tails as well

You're doing very well with your sentences! Just a tiny adjustment:

Instead of "I like when they go for their tails," you could say, "I like it when they chase their tails."

So your sentence would be: "I like playing with them; I like it when they chase their tails."

Dogs chasing their tails can be very funny to watch. Have you seen any other funny things dogs do in the park?
I remember that i could see one dog jumping in the grass

You're getting better! Here's a small correction: use "could" when you're talking about an ability you had in the past but not now. If you're talking about something that happened in the past, you use the simple past tense "saw."

So you would say, "I remember that I saw a dog jumping in the grass."

Jumping dogs are definitely a playful sight! Tell me, what do you feel when you see animals playing in the park?
I'm feelling glad and joy, its very relaxing to see that and enjoy the sight

You're almost there! Just a couple of tweaks:

The word "feelling" is a spelling mistake; it should be "feeling." Additionally, the word "joy" is often used as a noun, so the phrase "feeling glad and joy" should be "feeling glad and joyful."

So your corrected sentence is: "I'm feeling glad and joyful, it's very relaxing to see that and enjoy the sight."

I'm glad the park brings you peace and happiness. Do you also enjoy the sounds of the park, like the birds or the wind in the trees?
I love the sounds of birds, its very delightful that i can't describe in words, remembers me to other places

You're expressing yourself very nicely, and I understand the feeling you're trying to convey. Here are the slight modifications to make your sentences clearer:

Instead of "its," it should be "it's" (it is). And in the phrase "remembers me to other places," a better way to say it would be "it reminds me of other places."

So the corrected sentences are: "I love the sounds of birds; it's very delightful. I can't describe it in words; it reminds me of other places."

It's special how nature can trigger memories of different places. What other places does the park remind you of?
it reminds me of my older home, that house owner to my grandfather, I lived in that house many years of my youth

You conveyed a nostalgic feeling beautifully. Just a few corrections to make your sentences sound more natural:

- Instead of "that house owner to my grandfather," you should say "that house belonged to my grandfather."
- Instead of "I lived in that house many years of my youth," it's more natural to say "I spent many years of my youth in that house."

So your corrected sentences are: "It reminds me of my old home, the one that belonged to my grandfather. I spent many years of my youth in that house."

Your connection to the park and your memories is quite touching. Did the house have a big garden or was it near a park?
neither, that house it near a small place where there are a couple of trees and plants

You're doing well! Let's make a small correction and simplification to your sentence:

Instead of "that house it near," it should be "that house is near."

Also, "a small place where there are a couple of trees and plants" can be simplified to "a small green area with a few trees and plants."

So, your corrected sentence is: "Neither, that house is near a small green area with a few trees and plants."

It must have been nice to have that green space close by. Do you often visit similar places now to reminisce about the past?
unfortunately, i don't go out of my house often, i like to stay and relax in my house

You've expressed yourself very clearly. A slight correction to your sentence:

Instead of "I don't go out of my house often," a more natural way to say it in English would be, "I don't go out often," or "I don't leave my house very often."

So, your corrected sentence is: "Unfortunately, I don't go out often; I like to stay and relax in my home."

Relaxing at home is very important too. What do you enjoy doing at home to relax?
there are many activities that i do, one of my favorite is playing video games and surface the internet

Nice work! There are just two small things to correct:

1. The phrase "surface the internet" is not commonly used. Instead, you can say "surf the internet."
2. When listing activities, it's smoother to use "favorites" as you've talked about multiple activities.

So your sentence can be: "There are many activities that I do. One of my favorites is playing video games and surfing the internet."

It's great that you have hobbies that you can enjoy indoors. What types of video games do you like playing?
i like all of kinds videogames, specially the RPG, MOBA and games that tell a very interesting stories

You're doing really well! Just a slight adjustment for clarification and grammar:

Instead of "all of kinds videogames," it should be "all kinds of video games." The genres "RPG" (Role-Playing Games) and "MOBA" (Multiplayer Online Battle Arena) are fine but should have a "the" before them because you are mentioning specific types of games.

Also, in "games that tell a very interesting stories," the word "stories" should be singular after "a very."

So the correct sentence would be: "I like all kinds of video games, especially the RPG, MOBA, and games that tell very interesting stories."

RPGs and MOBAs are indeed popular for their immersive experiences. Do you have a favorite game you're playing right now?
yes, this game is Heroes of the storm, it's a MOBA created by Blizzard, this one is one of my favorites game

You're getting the hang of it, good job! Just a small correction on plurality:

Instead of "my favorites game," it should be "one of my favorite games" because "favorite" describes the singular "one," and "games" is the plural form which goes with "one of."

So your sentence would be: "Yes, I'm playing Heroes of the Storm. It's a MOBA created by Blizzard, and it's one of my favorite games."

"Heroes of the Storm" is indeed a popular game by Blizzard. What do you enjoy most about playing it?
with no doubt, the heroes of the different franchise like World of Warcraft, this last one is the another game that I enjoyful

You're doing well with expressing your thoughts, and I just have a couple of minor corrections for you:

Instead of "with no doubt," a more natural expression would be "without a doubt."

Regarding the phrase "the heroes of the different franchise like World of Warcraft," it would be more clear to say "the heroes from different Blizzard franchises, such as World of Warcraft."

Lastly, instead of "this last one is the another game that I enjoyful," it should be "the latter is another game that I enjoy."

Putting it all together:

"Without a doubt, I love the heroes from different Blizzard franchises, such as World of Warcraft. The latter is another game that I enjoy."

It's interesting how Heroes of the Storm brings together characters from various games. Do you have a favorite hero you like to play with?
